string-array.name: hints
string-array.item: "Supports up to 2953 UTF-8 encoded bytes.\n\nSupports multi-byte characters. If the content is too long, the code will be complex and affect recognition."
"Supports up to 2953 UTF-8 encoded bytes.\n\nSupports multi-byte characters. If the content is too long, the code will be complex and affect recognition."
"Supports up to 1777 UTF-8 encoded bytes.\n\nSupports multi-byte characters. If the content is too long, the code will be complex and affect recognition."
"Supports up to 2335 UTF-8 encoded bytes.\n\nOnly supports English characters. If the content is too long, the code will be complex and affect recognition."
"Supports 11-digit numeric input.\n\nOnly supports digits. Generates a code image containing 12 digits, including the last check digit."
"Supports 7-digit numeric input.\n\nThe first digit must be 0 or 1. Generates a code image containing 8 digits, including the last check digit."

"Supports up to 80-digit numeric input, and must be even digits.\n\nOnly supports digits. Generates a code image containing even digits. If the content is too long, the code will be complex and affect recognition."
"Supports 7-digit numeric input.\n\nOnly supports digits. Generates a code image containing 8 digits, including the last check digit."

"Supports 12-digit numeric input.\n\nOnly supports digits. The first digit cannot be 0. Generates a code image containing 13 digits, including the last check digit. If the first digit is not 0, a UPC-A code is generated."
"Supports up to 80 bytes in length.\n\nCharacter set can be digits, uppercase letters, and - . \$ / + % SPACE English format symbols."
"Supports up to 80 bytes in length.\n\nCharacter set can be digits, uppercase letters, and - . \$ / + % * SPACE English format symbols."
"Supports up to 80 bytes in length.\n\nCharacter set is the character set in the encoding table."

"Supports up to 2953 UTF-8 encoded bytes.\n\nIf the beginning is ABCDTN*E characters, the end must also be the corresponding characters. If not, A is added to the beginning and end by default. Other characters can be digits and - . \$ / : + English format symbols."
